name: github_create_update_file
description: Create or update a file in a GitHub repository. CRITICAL WORKFLOW: For updating existing files, you MUST first call github_get_file_sha or github_read_code to get the current file SHA, then EXTRACT the 'sha' field from that response and use it here. For new files, use empty string '' for SHA.

sha.description: SHA of the current file (REQUIRED). Get this first using github_read_code or github_get_file_sha. Use empty string '' for new files only.

path.description: Path to the file in the repository

content.description: File content (will be base64 encoded automatically)

message.description: Commit message for the file change

workflow: FOR UPDATING EXISTING FILES: STEP 1: Call github_get_file_sha or github_read_code with owner, repo, path, AND the SAME branch/ref you plan to update. STEP 2: Call github_create_update_file with that SHA, content, and SAME branch. CRITICAL: The SHA must be from the exact same branch you're updating! FOR NEW FILES: Call github_create_update_file with sha='' (empty string).

examples.name: Create new file
examples.description: For new files, use empty string for SHA

parameters.content:
# New Feature

This is a new feature documentation.

examples.name: Update existing file
examples.description: After getting current SHA from github_read_code
